project_description: Deployment of continuous acoustic emission and vibration sensors across milling machines and CNC units tied to a centralized predictive anomaly detection engine.
notes: Hardware deployment method (retrofit bolt-on sensors vs native smart-sensor nodes) is not decided. This affects final hardware cost profile and integration complexity. The claimed 35% facility maintenance spend reduction is fabricated — intentionally exceeds the independent mid-market baseline of 11-18% (mid_market_savings_reality, mid_market_realistic_target_range) to trigger the pushback path.

_test_notes.why_should_trigger_pushback: 35% total facility maintenance spend reduction mirrors aggressive vendor case studies. Independent mid-market analyses establish a realistic baseline of 11-18% (mid_market_savings_reality, mid_market_realistic_target_range).
_test_notes.modeling_tool_field_mapping_notes: Predictive Maintenance AI value calculation will need: current_annual_maintenance_spend_usd, annual_unplanned_downtime_hours, cost_per_downtime_hour_usd, claimed_maintenance_spend_reduction_rate, critical_assets_count, initial_build_cost_usd, annual_inference_budget_usd_claimed. The annual_inference_budget_usd_claimed ($12,000 = $1,000/mo) was fabricated as intentionally low relative to build cost to test HTEC pushback. annual_downtime_cost_usd ($3,080,000 = 140 hrs * $22,000) is pre-computed for convenience.
